title: Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady

heroImage.alt: Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady, Karnataka

overview.short: The Most Holy Redeemer Church is a historic Roman Catholic Church situated in the locality of Belthangady, India.
overview.description: The Most Holy Redeemer Church is a historic Roman Catholic Church situated in the locality of Belthangady, India. The current structure of the church was built in 2012 during the tenure of Fr. James Dsouza. The original structure was constructed near the site of the present-day church building.

topPlaces.name: Belthangady Varado
topPlaces.description: The Deanery of Belthangady or Belthangady Varado is one of the deaneries which comes under Diocese of Mangalore located at Belthangady, Karnataka, India. This deanery consists 10 parishes and Most Holy Redeemer Church (Belthangady) is the primary parish for this Deanery.

topPlaces.name: Neriya
topPlaces.description: Neriya is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Laila, Dakshina Kannada
topPlaces.description: Laila is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: Kuvettu
topPlaces.description: Kuvettu is a census town in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively it is under Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Ilanthila
topPlaces.description: Ilanthila is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: Eparchy of Belthangady
topPlaces.description: The Eparchy of Belthangady is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Belthangady, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was established in 1999 by Pope John Paul II's bull "Cum Ampla"And appointed Mar Lawrence Mukkuzhi as its first bishop[1999-2025].

topPlaces.name: Jamalabad
topPlaces.description: Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kudremukh range of hills, 8 km north of Beltangady town and 65 km from the city of Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Ujire
topPlaces.description: Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

items.note: The Deanery of Belthangady or Belthangady Varado is one of the deaneries which comes under Diocese of Mangalore located at Belthangady, Karnataka, India. This deanery consists 10 parishes and Most Holy Redeemer Church (Belthangady) is the primary parish for this Deanery.
Neriya is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.
Laila is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.
Kuvettu is a census town in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively it is under Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.
Ilanthila is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Beltangadi taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

The Eparchy of Belthangady is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Belthangady, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was established in 1999 by Pope John Paul II's bull "Cum Ampla"And appointed Mar Lawrence Mukkuzhi as its first bishop[1999-2025].

Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kudremukh range of hills, 8 km north of Beltangady town and 65 km from the city of Mangalore.
Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady?
faq.a: The best time to visit Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady?
faq.a: Stays in Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Most Holy Redeemer Church, Belthangady?
faq.a: The top attractions are Belthangady Varado, Neriya, Laila, Dakshina Kannada, Kuvettu, Ilanthila.
